name: Security Diagram Evaluation Rubric
description: Evaluation criteria for security diagrams (DFD, attack chain, sequence, attack tree).

categories.name: Trust Boundaries
categories.description: Clear identification and marking of trust boundaries

options.label: Pass
criteria: All trust boundaries clearly marked with dashed lines or containers
Boundary types labeled (network, process, sandbox, origin)
Boundary violations highlighted for attack flows

options.label: Partial
criteria: Key trust boundaries marked and labeled
Some relevant boundaries may be missing

options.label: Fail
criteria: Trust boundaries missing in security diagram

categories.evaluationPrompt: Check trust boundaries. Are all boundaries clearly marked? Are they labeled with type? Are violations highlighted?

categories.name: Attack Flow Clarity
categories.description: Clear representation of attack steps and sequence

options.label: Pass
criteria: Attack flows visually distinct from normal flows

categories.evaluationPrompt: Evaluate attack flow clarity. Are steps numbered? Are outcomes shown? Are attack flows visually distinct?

categories.name: Notation Standards

options.label: Pass
criteria: Uses correct DFD notation (circles=processes, rectangles=external entities)
Attack trees use standard AND/OR notation

options.label: Partial
criteria: Minor deviations that don't impair understanding

categories.evaluationPrompt: Check notation standards. Does the diagram use standard DFD/UML notation? Are shapes used consistently?

categories.name: Data Flow Accuracy

options.label: Pass
criteria: All flows labeled with protocol (HTTPS, WebSocket, etc.)

categories.evaluationPrompt: Check data flow accuracy. Are flows labeled with protocols? Are directions correct? Are data types indicated?

categories.name: Consistency
categories.description: Consistency with accompanying article and threat model

options.label: Pass
criteria: Element IDs match threat model JSON
No contradictions between diagram and text

categories.evaluationPrompt: Check consistency. Do element IDs match the threat model? Does the attack flow match the article narrative?

categories.name: Rendering Quality

options.label: Pass
criteria: Clean layout with no overlapping elements

options.label: Partial
criteria: Renders but with minor layout issues

categories.evaluationPrompt: Evaluate rendering quality. Does the diagram render correctly? Is the layout clean and readable?
